title: Sega Genesis Platform Stack
body:
# Sega Genesis Platform Stack

**Platform stack:** `console_genesis` · **Era:** 1988–1997 · **Vendor:** Sega · **Count:** 9

Motorola 68000 main CPU with Z80 sound sub-CPU, Yamaha FM, and Sega VDP — the 16-bit cartridge platform launched **October 1988** in Japan (Mega Drive).

## Stack doctrine

This page lists every combinatorics leaf stamped `platform_stack: console_genesis` plus companion dies emitted from `field-cpu-companion-seed.json`. Host and guest CPUs matching the stack's `match` rules receive `always_with` linkage at combinatorics build — no manual wiring.

## Manufacturing notes — companion dies

- **Sega 315-5313 VDP** (Sega, 16-bit) — Video Display Processor — scroll planes + sprites
- **Yamaha YM2612 FM** (Yamaha, 8-bit) — 6-channel FM synth — Genesis stereo audio
- **Genesis Z80 Sound Glue** (Sega, 8-bit) — Sub-CPU bus arbiter — Z80 ↔ 68000 sound path
- **Sega 315-5304 I/O** (Sega, 16-bit) — Controller port + expansion — Genesis I/O ASIC
- **Genesis TMSS Security** (Sega, 16-bit) — Trademark Security System — boot lock handshake

## Catalog ids

The `chip_ids` array holds all **9** entries for this platform stack, sorted alphabetically. Run `field-ironclad-chips-combinatorics.py combinatronic` for path percents and imprint renders.

*Stack: console_genesis · seed: data/field-cpu-companion-seed.json · citation: ironclad:chips:3*
